turns.content: man its been raining here all week and nobody is coming in
That's rough. Rain weeks are brutal for auto shops — people just don't want to deal with it unless they absolutely have to. Are you seeing a total dropoff or just way below your usual traffic?
total dropoff. monday I had two cars. two. tuesday was three. today I sent one of my guys home early because there was nothing to do

Sending guys home early hurts on both sides — they lose hours and you still have overhead running. A week like that can really dent the month.

Any chance some of those customers are just delayed and will pile in once it clears up? That's usually the silver lining — you get slammed the week after.
thats what I keep telling myself. but the forecast says rain through friday too so thats basically the whole week gone

A full week is painful. That's real revenue just evaporating.

One thing you could do if you've got the downtime — use it for stuff that usually gets pushed aside. Clean up the bays, organize inventory, knock out any maintenance on your equipment. At least then the dead time isn't completely wasted.
yeah we been doing some of that. organized the back room this morning. but I tell you what, its hard to feel productive when the register aint ringing

I hear you. Busy work doesn't pay the bills. There's a real difference between keeping your hands moving and actually making money.

Have you thought about reaching out to any customers who are overdue for service? Even just a few phone calls or texts — "hey, you're due for a rotation" type thing. Might pull a couple people in even with the rain.
I dont really have a system for that. I got peoples numbers in my phone from when they called but thats about it

That's a start though. Even scrolling through your recent call log and texting five or six people could drum up a job or two. Something like "Hey, just checking in — let me know if you need anything before spring hits."

No system needed for that. Just your phone and ten minutes.
might do that tomorrow if its dead again. I just hate feeling like Im begging for work

It's not begging — it's just reminding people you exist. Big chains spend thousands on that. You're doing it for free with a text message.

But I get the feeling. Nobody wants to chase business. The rain will pass and people will come back in. This time of year is usually when things start picking up anyway.
yeah youre right. just venting I guess. sitting here watching it pour outside staring at empty bays

Nothing wrong with venting. A slow week with rain and overhead costs ticking — that'll get to anybody.

Hang in there. Once this clears up you'll probably have a backlog to deal with and be wishing for a slow day again.
ha. probably true. alright appreciate it man
Anytime. Hope the sun comes out soon — for your sanity and your register.
